graph.name: Small Diesel Power Plant
graph.description: A simplified representation of a diesel-driven electrical power generation plant including prime mover, generator, protection, control, and distribution components.

properties.name: Diesel Prime Mover
properties.description: The diesel internal combustion engine that acts as the prime mover. It converts chemical energy from fuel into mechanical rotation that drives the alternator shaft.

properties.name: Synchronous Alternator
properties.description: The three-phase synchronous generator mechanically coupled to the diesel engine. Converts mechanical rotation into AC electrical energy.

properties.name: Automatic Voltage Regulator
properties.description: The AVR continuously monitors the alternator output voltage and adjusts the excitation current to maintain stable output voltage under varying load conditions.

properties.name: Electronic Speed Governor
properties.description: Controls the fuel rack position of the diesel engine to maintain constant rotational speed (and therefore frequency) regardless of load changes.

properties.name: Main Generator Circuit Breaker
properties.description: The main protection switch between the alternator output and the main bus bar. Trips automatically on fault conditions such as short circuit or overload.

properties.name: Step-Up Distribution Transformer
properties.description: Steps up the generator voltage from 400V to 11kV for more efficient distribution over longer distances to the load centers.

properties.name: 11kV Main Distribution Bus
properties.description: The central distribution point at 11kV where the transformer secondary connects and distributes power to different feeders and loads.

properties.name: Generator Control Panel (GCP)
properties.description: The master control unit that monitors all plant parameters (voltage, current, frequency, temperature, pressure), manages automatic start/stop sequences, and provides alarms and fault logging.

properties.name: Feeder A - Essential Loads
properties.description: Distribution feeder supplying essential/critical loads (lighting, control systems, safety equipment) that must remain energized at all times.

properties.name: Feeder B - Industrial Loads
properties.description: Distribution feeder supplying industrial loads (motors, HVAC, process equipment) that can be shed during low generation capacity.
The diesel engine crankshaft is directly coupled to the alternator rotor shaft via a flexible disc coupling, transmitting mechanical torque.
The AVR senses the alternator terminal voltage and controls the excitation winding current to regulate output voltage.
The AVR outputs a controlled DC current to the alternator field winding to adjust magnetic flux and thus output voltage.
The governor controls the fuel injection rack of the diesel engine to maintain constant speed and frequency.
A magnetic pickup sensor on the engine flywheel sends speed pulses to the governor as feedback.
Three-phase AC power flows from the alternator terminals to the main circuit breaker.
When the main breaker is closed, power flows to the step-up transformer primary winding.
Stepped-up 11kV power from the transformer secondary feeds the main distribution bus bar.
The main bus distributes power to the essential loads feeder.
The main bus distributes power to the industrial loads feeder.
The GCP monitors engine parameters (speed, oil pressure, coolant temperature) via ECU and sends start/stop commands.
The GCP measures alternator output voltage, current, frequency, kW, kVAr and power factor via instrument transformers.
The GCP sends open/close commands to the main circuit breaker based on load conditions and protection logic.
The GCP adjusts the governor speed setpoint for load sharing and frequency fine-tuning.
The GCP adjusts the AVR voltage setpoint for reactive power control and voltage fine-tuning.
